findings.ref: tests/guard-r10-port.tests.ps1 (corpus) vs requirements.md Risks / design.md Test Strategy #2
findings.note: The parity corpus does not exercise every protected-suffix class, which requirements.md designates as the mandatory mitigation for the Critical incomplete-port risk and design.md promises ('decision equality with .py/.js across every protected-suffix class'). Zero fixtures exist for: kill-switch.{js,sh,ps1}; the reviewer-agent files; validate_path.py; check-evidence-bundle.*; .claude/settings.local.json; and, most importantly, all three ProtectedGatePluginJsonSuffixes entries, which are matched by a distinct code branch in Test-IsProtectedGateFile (leading-slash/TrimStart handling) and feed the ProtectedBasenames fail-closed fallback, so an error in that branch of the staged .ps1 would ship undetected on Windows. AC-001 ('any path in the protected table') is evidenced for only ~7 of 31 table entries.

The Impl-Review-Status forgery corpus exercises only the write-tool content path. The edit (new_string) and multiedit (edits[]) branches of Test-ImplReviewStatusPassedIncreases in the staged .ps1 are untested, and no shell/apply_patch forgery scenario establishes parity with the .py/.js twins for those payload shapes.

The ASCII/no-BOM check defaults to the staged human-copy artifact; the live enforcement path is checked only when GUARD_PS1 is explicitly set. The bundle also carries no evidence that the check is registered in the repository test suite (tests/run-all.sh is not in the artifact set), so the AC-015 clause 'runs in the test suite' cannot be verified from the bundle alone. Mitigated at copy time by the SHA-256 manifest, so Minor.

For mv, only the final non-flag argument (destination) is treated as a write; the mv SOURCE is treated as a read even though mv destroys it at the source path. If this mirrors the .py/.js twins it is a shared pre-existing heuristic gap rather than a port divergence, but twin behavior for this case is not verifiable from the bundle.

Sanitization redacted load-bearing security constants and several regex literals in the staged guard source, so exact parity of these values with the .py/.js twins cannot be reviewed from the bundle; assurance for those code paths rests entirely on the 25-scenario decision-parity corpus and the RED/GREEN logs. Future bundles should escape rather than redact these values.
